description: Real human-in-the-loop approval flow — pre-flight policy check in bridge, expensive tool on server, retry-after-approval mechanism.

userStories.title: Add tool_args column to DB schema and pending approval query
userStories.description: Add tool_args TEXT column to payments table in db.ts. Add getPendingApproval(id) function that returns a single pending_approval row by ID. Update PaymentRecord type in types.ts to include toolArgs field.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/agent-wallet/src/types.ts PaymentRecord has toolArgs?: string field

packages/agent-wallet/src/db.ts has ALTER TABLE migration adding tool_args column (use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with column included, since SQLite doesn't support ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS cleanly — just add it to the CREATE TABLE statement)
packages/agent-wallet/src/db.ts exports getPendingApproval(id: number): PaymentRecord | null
getPendingApproval queries WHERE id = ? AND status = 'approved'
insertPayment accepts optional toolArgs in the record object and stores it
mapPaymentRow includes toolArgs from the row
pnpm --filter @swigpay/agent-wallet run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Since the DB might already exist from previous runs, the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS won't add the new column. Handle this by also running ALTER TABLE payments ADD COLUMN tool_args TEXT DEFAULT '' in a try/catch (ignore error if column exists). Read db.ts carefully — the schema is in getDb() function around line 53.

userStories.title: Add expensive_tool to MCP server at approval threshold price
userStories.description: Add a new paid tool 'expensive_tool' to the MCP server that returns mock expensive analysis data. Price it at $0.50 USDC which matches the default approvalThresholdUsdc, so it triggers the approval gate.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/mcp-server/src/server.ts has new tool 'expensive_tool' registered
Tool description mentions it costs $0.50 USDC and requires human approval
Tool is wrapped with wrapWithPayment (same as solana_price/account_info)
Tool returns JSON with fields: analysis, confidence, timestamp
GET /health endpoint includes 'expensive_tool (paid, requires approval)' in tools list
pnpm --filter @swigpay/mcp-server run typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tool needs its own price constant since it's different from the other tools ($0.50 vs $0.001). Add EXPENSIVE_TOOL_PRICE_USD = 0.50 to apps/mcp-server/src/config.ts. Create a separate paymentAccepts for this tool using the higher price. You'll need to create a second wrapWithPayment instance or pass the price dynamically. Simplest approach: create a second resourceServer.buildPaymentRequirements call with the higher price and a second wrapWithPayment. Read server.ts lines 43-61 for the pattern.

userStories.title: Bridge pre-flight policy check and retry-approved subcommand
userStories.description: Modify call-tool in the bridge to run enforceSpendPolicy BEFORE connecting to MCP. If requiresHumanApproval, insert pending_approval record with tool_args and return pending status JSON without calling MCP. Add new 'retry-approved <id>' subcommand that reads the approved record, calls the tool bypassing threshold, and returns result.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/agent-zero-bridge.ts call-tool expensive_tool returns JSON with status:'pending_approval', paymentId, reason — WITHOUT calling MCP server
scripts/agent-zero-bridge.ts call-tool ping returns normal result (under threshold, no change to existing behavior)
scripts/agent-zero-bridge.ts call-tool solana_price returns normal result (under threshold, no change)
scripts/agent-zero-bridge.ts retry-approved <id> reads approved record from DB, calls MCP tool, returns JSON with status:'approved', result, paymentMade:true, txHash, explorerUrl
retry-approved with non-approved or non-existent id returns error to stderr
Pending approval record includes tool_args (JSON string of the args passed to call-tool)
retry-approved parses tool_args from the record to pass to callTool
For retry-approved, the approvalThresholdUsdc is set to 9999 to bypass the threshold check in x402client's onPaymentRequested
pnpm --filter @swigpay/agent-wallet run typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
This is the core story. Key implementation details:
1. Import enforceSpendPolicy from @swigpay/agent-wallet
2. Build SpendPolicy from env vars (dailyLimitUsdc, perTxLimitUsdc, approvalThresholdUsdc, whitelistedEndpoints)
3. For call-tool: run enforceSpendPolicy first. If approved=true, proceed as normal. If requiresHumanApproval=true, insert pending_approval record with tool name + tool_args (JSON.stringify the args), return pending JSON. If approved=false (hard reject), return rejected JSON.
4. For retry-approved: call getPendingApproval(id). If null or not approved, error. Otherwise, parse tool_args, create client with modified config (approvalThresholdUsdc: 9999), call the tool, return result.
5. The MCP_PRICE_PER_CALL_USD env var is $0.001 but expensive_tool is $0.50. The bridge needs to know the price of the tool it's calling. Simplest: hardcode a map {expensive_tool: 0.50} in the bridge, default everything else to MCP_PRICE_PER_CALL_USD from env.
6. Read spendPolicy.ts for the exact function signature. Read x402client.ts lines 70-111 for how onPaymentRequested works — when retry-approved bypasses threshold, the onPaymentRequested in x402client will still check the policy. So we need to either: (a) modify agentConfig.approvalThresholdUsdc to 9999 before creating the client, or (b) add a bypass flag. Option (a) is simpler — just override the config value when creating the client for retry.
7. The insertPayment for pending_approval should use the new toolArgs field from US-A1.
